Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely chronic kidney disease with possible nephrotic-range proteinuria. The abnormal urine appearance and color may indicate underlying urinary tract or hepatic pathology. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria; consider possible analytical error or altered red cell turnover. Clinical correlation and further nephrological and metabolic evaluation are recommended.
